candidates.headline: Probate online to go live for solicitors nationwide on the Courts Portal from 2 June 2026
candidates.factual_basis: The Courts Service said solicitors nationwide would be able to apply for grants of probate online through the Courts Portal from 2 June, after a Dublin pilot received more than 100 applications and led to system updates based on solicitor feedback.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A direct Irish court-modernisation item affecting probate practices, e-filing adoption, solicitor workflows, turnaround times, and the wider Courts Service 10-year modernisation programme.

candidates.headline: High Court issues Practice Direction HC141 on mediation and ADR compliance
candidates.factual_basis: The Courts Service published notice that the High Court had issued Practice Direction HC141, highlighting Mediation Act 2017 obligations and warning practitioners and litigants about consequences for non-compliance.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to Irish litigation operations because it changes how teams evidence ADR compliance, manage case strategy, advise clients, and control costs risk in High Court work.

candidates.headline: Dublin Circuit Court personal insolvency remote callover set for 8 June 2026
candidates.factual_basis: The Courts Service notified practitioners of a remote callover to confirm readiness for Dublin Circuit Court personal insolvency hearings listed for 10 and 11 June 2026.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A narrow but concrete example of remote procedural management continuing in Irish civil practice and affecting practitioner scheduling and court-readiness workflows.

candidates.headline: Law Society opens free Social Media, AI and the Law MOOC with more than 3,000 registrations
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that registration opened for the Law Society's free on-demand course on social media, AI and law, starting on 9 June 2026 with more than 3,000 participants already registered.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows strong Irish demand for legal education on AI, social-media liability, privacy, data protection, cybercrime, ecommerce, digital manipulation, and responsible online practice.

candidates.headline: Irish co-founded legal AI startup Wordsmith raises USD 70 million Series B
candidates.factual_basis: Irish Legal News reported Wordsmith AI's USD 70 million Series B, backed by Highland Europe and Index Ventures, and said Dublin-born COO Robbie Falkenthal plans to build out an Irish presence.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A high-priority Irish-linked legal AI funding story focused on in-house legal intake, workflow routing, outside-counsel reduction, activity measurement, and Irish customer and talent expansion.

candidates.headline: Survey sends clear message on flat-fee plan for District Court criminal legal aid
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported a Law Society survey in which 70% of criminal-law solicitors said they would be unlikely or very unlikely to continue District Court criminal legal-aid work under the proposed flat-fee model.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A major Irish access-to-justice and service-delivery story because fee design could affect solicitor availability, vulnerable defendant representation, delay, and the practical economics of District Court defence work.

candidates.headline: More than 150 criminal-law solicitors voice anger and frustration over flat-fee proposals
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that more than 150 solicitors attended a Law Society information meeting on 9 June, where speakers warned that the proposed model could cause practitioners to stop taking District Court legal-aid work.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Useful follow-up on the same Irish criminal legal-aid issue, showing practitioner mobilisation and unresolved operational concerns before the proposed 1 July change.

candidates.headline: Public consultation opened to inform review of Ireland's Disability Act 2005
candidates.factual_basis: The Department of Children, Disability and Equality opened a public consultation on reviewing the Disability Act 2005 in light of Ireland's UNCRPD obligations, with submissions due by 9 September 2026.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Potentially important for accessible public services, universal design, rights advice, assessment-of-need processes, public law litigation, and legal service delivery for disabled people.

candidates.headline: Deemed Insolvent Process widens access to Ireland's Insolvency Payments Scheme
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported commencement of the Deemed Insolvent Process, expanding Insolvency Payments Scheme access where an employer has ceased trading without formal liquidation, receivership, or bankruptcy.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Employment, insolvency, and litigation practitioners need to advise workers and employers on the new claims pathway, safeguards, and the distinction from formal insolvency status.

candidates.headline: Eighty per cent of Northern Ireland crime victims have no confidence in justice system
candidates.factual_basis: Irish Legal News reported Queen's University Belfast researchers saying more than 80% of Northern Ireland crime victims are not confident they will receive justice, after an event involving policymakers, lawyers, psychologists, the Lady Chief Justice, and the Victims' Commissioner Designate.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: All-island relevance for victim-centred service design, trauma-informed justice, practitioner training, court communication, and evidence-based justice-system reform.

candidates.headline: ICCL raises concerns over reported nationwide garda bodycam rollout
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported ICCL concern about reports of a EUR 150 million nationwide garda body-worn camera rollout before publication of the pilot assessment report or a finalised code of practice.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to criminal defence, policing oversight, surveillance governance, data protection, public consultation standards, and digital-evidence workflows in Irish criminal justice.

candidates.headline: EU Justice Scoreboard shows Ireland high on trust but low on some justice-system digital indicators
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that the 2026 EU Justice Scoreboard ranked Ireland in the top half for public online justice information but fourth from bottom for procedural rules allowing digital technology in court.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Gives Irish readers comparative evidence on court digital readiness, ADR incentives, justice-system efficiency, judge resources, and where modernisation pressure may fall next.

candidates.headline: Pinsents' botched AI use sparks dependency alarm
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that Pinsent Masons had referred itself to the SRA after misleading the court twice in Cork v Smith: once with AI-hallucinated references and again with an AI-produced explanatory letter that remained inaccurate.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A practical AI-governance warning for firms adopting internal AI search or drafting tools, especially around supervision, verification, junior-lawyer training, and duties not to mislead the court.

candidates.headline: UK government plans AI legal assistants for Crown Court backlog pilot
candidates.factual_basis: The Guardian reported that David Lammy would announce AI-powered virtual legal assistants for Crown courts, with judges planning tools to identify trial-ready cases and group similar hearings, while the Law Society urged transparent evaluation and safeguards.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A major UK justice-innovation story for courts, criminal practitioners, legal-aid policy, AI governance, backlog management, and Irish comparison with court digitisation efforts.

candidates.headline: UK Government launches AI Growth Lab with legal as first focus area
candidates.factual_basis: Legal IT Insider reported that the UK Government's advisory AI Growth Lab will first focus on LawTech, legal services, and conveyancing, with the ICO working with the CLC, SRA, and Legal Services Board on cross-regulatory AI challenges.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Important for legal AI builders and adopters because regulators are trying to clarify how existing rules apply to legal-service AI while supporting responsible adoption and access to justice.

candidates.headline: AI Act enforcement gets independent expert support
candidates.factual_basis: The European Commission appointed a Scientific Panel and an Advisory Forum to support AI Act enforcement, including GPAI model classification, systemic risks, evaluation methods, standardisation, and implementation challenges.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: EU AI governance architecture is becoming operational, shaping legal advice on AI suppliers, legal AI tools, compliance evidence, auditability, and AI-risk controls for regulated clients.

candidates.headline: Commission seeks feedback on draft trusted flaggers guidelines under the Digital Services Act
candidates.factual_basis: The Commission opened feedback on draft DSA trusted-flagger guidelines, noting that more than 70 trusted flaggers have been designated and naming Bank of Ireland as an example focused on financial scams.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to Irish and EU online-safety, fraud, IP, platform-moderation, content-dispute, and compliance work, with a direct Bank of Ireland angle for Irish legal readers.

candidates.headline: Commission fines Temu EUR 200 million for breaching the Digital Services Act
candidates.factual_basis: The Commission issued a EUR 200 million DSA fine to Temu, saying the platform failed to properly identify, analyse, and assess systemic risks from illegal products and resulting consumer harm in the EU.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A significant DSA enforcement action for platform, consumer, product-safety, investigations, regulatory disputes, and online-marketplace legal teams advising clients on risk assessments and evidence.

candidates.headline: Simmons & Simmons to reward AI adoption in bonus overhaul
candidates.factual_basis: Financial News reported that Simmons & Simmons will allow associates to count up to 50 bonus-eligible hours for meaningful generative-AI work that benefits clients or the firm, with AI champions able to count the full 100-hour allocation.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows a UK/international law firm embedding AI adoption into incentives, knowledge management, client work, legal engineering, and professional-performance systems rather than treating AI use as optional experimentation.

candidates.headline: DeepIP acquires PatentMaker to target European leadership in patent AI
candidates.factual_basis: LawSites reported that AI patent drafting platform DeepIP acquired PatentMaker, a German patent workflow tool, and would open a German office inside Boehmert & Boehmert's Munich premises.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Signals consolidation in European IP AI tooling and possible workflow change across invention capture, prior-art search, drafting, filing, prosecution, and post-grant patent work.

candidates.headline: Sandstone raises USD 30 million for AI-native in-house legal teams
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported Sandstone's USD 30 million Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners for a platform connecting counterparties, stakeholders, matters, obligations, contracts, and history for in-house teams.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows investor backing for legal relationship management, intake, triage, drafting, review, knowledge retrieval, and AI-native infrastructure for general counsel and legal operations.

candidates.headline: Kirkland and Palantir partner for private equity platform
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Kirkland & Ellis and Palantir launched a proprietary enterprise platform for private equity fundraising as part of a multiyear partnership using Palantir's AI Platform.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A high-end legal-service delivery story showing a major firm productising senior-lawyer judgment, institutional knowledge, and complex fund-formation workflows across more than 1,000 investment-funds lawyers.

candidates.headline: Legora buys Cadastral agentic real estate platform
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Legora acquired Cadastral, a 10-person AI agent platform for commercial real estate, and planned to establish a New York engineering hub.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows legal AI platforms expanding into adjacent transaction-heavy verticals where due diligence, leases, underwriting, zoning, refinancing, disputes, and document review drive legal workflows.

candidates.headline: LawGeex founders launch Superlegal NewMod firm
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that LawGeex co-founder Noory Bechor and Ilan Admon launched a regulated AI-first Superlegal law firm in Utah focused on construction contracting for SMBs, with attorney sign-off on reviews.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to alternative business structures, AI-enabled legal-service delivery, attorney-in-the-loop contracting, fixed-fee review, and competition between legal tech platforms and traditional firms.

candidates.headline: Ironclad founder Jason Boehmig joins OpenAI for legal vertical launch
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Ironclad founder Jason Boehmig joined OpenAI to lead product for the legal vertical and said OpenAI plans a legally focused range of tools.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Material for law firms, in-house teams, and legal tech vendors because it suggests another frontier AI provider is moving directly into legal workflow products and legal-market distribution.

candidates.headline: AmLaw 200 firm Hanson Bridgett goes all-in with Claude
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Hanson Bridgett adopted Claude firm-wide for attorneys and professional staff, using it across document review, drafting, research, organisation, internal operations, due diligence, and knowledge management.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A concrete law-firm AI adoption example involving general models, legal add-ons, enterprise data protections, written AI policy, workflow review, and change management for professional staff as well as lawyers.

candidates.headline: Claude for Legal has over 90 AI agents
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Claude for Legal has more than 90 named legal AI agents listed on GitHub, including workflow agents for vendor agreement review, DSAR response, termination review, and claim-chart building.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows legal AI moving from broad copilots to specific, customisable workflow agents that firms, in-house teams, clinics, and law schools may adapt to their own processes and safeguards.

candidates.headline: Lakshmikumaran & Sridharan Attorneys uses Dell AI legal assistant built with Dell AI Factory
candidates.factual_basis: The Times of India reported Dell's announcement that Indian law firm Lakshmikumaran & Sridharan Attorneys is using an AI legal assistant built with Dell AI Factory and Nvidia to access proprietary legal knowledge, regulatory updates, case law, and statutes.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A global law-firm AI adoption item showing enterprise AI infrastructure, proprietary knowledge retrieval, secure deployment, and a shift from research-heavy workflows to intelligence-led practice support.

candidates.headline: Ninth Circuit suspends two California lawyers over AI-generated false citations
candidates.factual_basis: The San Francisco Chronicle reported that the 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals fined two lawyers USD 2,500 each and suspended them for six months after briefs contained fictitious AI-generated case citations.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A concrete professional-risk item for litigators and AI adopters, reinforcing duties of competence, diligence, candour, supervision, and human verification of AI-assisted legal research.
